title: Iran's Resilient Oil Exports and Emerging Pressures in 2025
section_path: Iran's Resilient Oil Exports and Emerging Pressures in 2025
section_path_text: Iran's Resilient Oil Exports and Emerging Pressures in 2025
summary:
Main
Tanker - Weekly Market Monitor
Snapshot of Crude and Product Freight Rates, Supply-Demand
Week 17, 25 April, 2025
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-04-25 112054.png | assets/2025-04-25_irans-resilient-oil-exports-and-emerging-pressures-in-2025_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_cead058ca464.png
Despite persistent international sanctions, Iran managed to sustain significant crude oil exports between January 2023 and March 2025, totaling approximately 268.5 million barrels. This continued export activity-entirely sourced from Iranian production-demonstrates Tehran's resilience and strategic adaptability in circumventing global pressure. A striking feature of Iran's export strategy is its overwhelming reliance on Kharg Island, which accounted for 96.6% of all shipments and 95.3% of terminal usage during this period.
